Forced to Eat Mango Frontend Engineer

We've spent the last few posts testing how to create records, so now it's time to move on to editing. This post walks through writing Mocha tests for updating MongoDB data with Mongoose — using set(), update(), findByIdAndUpdate(), and findOneAndUpdate(). I'll also point out a subtle mistake in how you assert that an update actually worked.

The Mango-Eating Frontend Dev

After writing a ton of Mongoose, it hit me that I'd skipped over MongoDB's Update Operators — and honestly that part matters a lot. So here's a make-up chapter covering Update Operators in MongoDB, including the handy $inc operator for incrementing and decrementing fields.
/en/nodejs/2022/03/06/mongoose-middleware-19/
Mongoose Has Middleware Too? Hooking Into save with pre and post
middleware

Hold on — Mongoose has Middleware too! Middleware is basically a middle layer: before you enter or do something, you have to pass through it first. And Mongoose's Middleware turns out to be really handy. In this post I'll show you how to hook into events like save with pre and post.
